params.name: offset
params.description: The number of documents to skip before starting to collect the result set.

params.name: limit
params.description: The numbers of results to return.

params.name: search
params.description: Partial, case-insensitive match with file name or tag name.

params.name: ordering
params.description: Sort the result set. A "-" at the beginning denotes DESC sort, e.g. -created_dt. Sort by multiple fields is supported. Supported values include: 'file_name', 'extractor', 'created_dt', 'validated_dt', 'archived_dt' and 'parsed__<dataPointSlug>'.

params.name: include_data
params.description: By default, this endpoint returns only the meta data of the documents. Set this to `true` will return a summary of the data that was parsed. If you want to retrieve the full set of data for a document, use the `GET /documents/{identifier}` endpoint.

params.name: exclude
params.description: Exclude some documents from the result.

params.name: in_review
params.description: Exclude documents that are currently being reviewed.

params.name: custom_identifier
params.description: Filter for documents with this custom identifier.

params.name: compact
params.description: If "true", the response is compacted to annotations' parsed data. Annotations' meta data are excluded. Default is "false".

params.name: count
params.description: If "false", the documents count is not computed, thus saving time for large collections. Default is "true".

params.name: snake_case
params.description: Whether to return the response in snake_case instead of camelCase. Default is false.

items.docs: Returns all the document summaries for that user, limited to 300 per page.

params.name: snake_case
params.description: Whether to return the response in snake_case instead of camelCase. Default is false.

params.name: format
params.description: Specify which format you want the response to be. Default is "json"

params.name: organization
params.description: Organization to report usage for. The caller must be an admin or owner.

params.name: workspace
params.description: Optionally scope the report to a single workspace within the organization.

params.name: document_type
params.description: Optionally scope the report to a single document type within the organization.

params.name: start
params.description: Inclusive start date of the report (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to 365 days before `end`.

params.name: end
params.description: Inclusive end date of the report (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to today.

items.docs:
Returns daily credits consumption for an organization over a date range. Optionally scope the report to a single workspace or document type.
`workspace` and `document_type` cannot be combined: usage data is aggregated independently along each axis, so a per-(workspace × document type) slice is not available.

info.name: Get list of all resthook subscriptions

params.name: offset
params.description: The number of documents to skip before starting to collect the result set.

params.name: limit
params.description: The numbers of results to return.

items.docs: Returns your resthook subscriptions.

After a subscription is successfully created, we'll send a POST request to your target URL with a `X-Hook-Secret` header.
You need to response to this request with a 200 status code to confirm your subscribe intention.
Then, you need to use the `X-Hook-Secret` to activate the subscription using the [/resthook_subscriptions/activate](#post-/v3/resthook_subscriptions/activate) endpoint.
For more information and detailed instructions, [see our webhooks documentation here.](http
Return a specific resthook subscription.
Update data of a resthook subscription.
Deletes the specified resthook subscription from the database.

After creating a subscription, we'll send a POST request to your target URL with a `X-Hook-Secret` header.
You should response to this with a 200 status code, and use the value of the `X-Hook-Secret` header that you received to activate the subscription using this endpoint.

info.name: Create a document from raw data

params.name: snake_case
params.description: Whether to return the response in snake_case instead of camelCase. Default is false.

items.docs:
Creates a document directly from structured resume or job description data for use in Search & Match functionality. This endpoint is specifically designed for programmatic document creation without file uploads.
If you want to upload a document with a file or URL, see the main [POST /v3/documents](#post-/v3/documents) endpoint under the Documents tag.
When successful, returns an `identifier` in the response for subsequent use with the [/documents/{identifier}](#get-/v3/documents/-iden

Update data of a document.
Only applicable for resumes and job descriptions. For other document types, please use the `PATCH /annotations/{id}` endpoint or the `POST /annotations/batch_update` endpoint.
Returns the list of searchable occupation groups.

params.name: offset
params.description: The number of documents to skip before starting to collect the result set.

params.name: limit
params.description: The numbers of results to return.

items.docs:
Searches through parsed resumes. Users have 3 options to create a search:

1. Match to a job description - a parsed job description is used to find candidates that suit it
2. Match to a resume - a parsed resume is used to find other candidates that have similar attributes
3. Search using custom criteria

Users should only populate 1 of jobDescription, resume or the custom criteria.

info.name: Get search result of specific resume

items.docs:
This contains more detailed information about the matching score of the search criteria, or which search criteria is missing in this resume.
The `identifier` is the unique ID returned via the [/resume_search](#post-/resume_search) endpoint.

info.name: Get the config for the logged in user's embeddable resume search tool

items.docs: Return configurations such as which fields can be displayed in the logged in user's embeddable resume search tool, what are their weights, what is the maximum number of results that can be returned, etc.

info.name: Update the config for the logged in user's embeddable resume search tool

items.docs: Update configurations such as which fields can be displayed in the logged in user's embeddable resume search tool, what are their weights, what is the maximum number of results that can be returned, etc.

info.name: Create a signed URL for the embeddable resume search tool

items.docs: Create and return a signed URL of the resume search tool which then can be embedded on a web page. An optional parameter `config_override` can be passed to override the user-level configurations of the embeddable resume search tool.

info.name: Match a single resume and job description

params.name: job_description
params.description: Identify the job description to match.

params.name: index
params.description: Optionally, specify an index to search in. If not specified, will search in all indexes.

params.name: search_expression
params.description: Add keywords to the search criteria.

params.name: job_titles_weight
params.description: How important is this criteria to the matching score, range from 0 to 1.

params.name: years_experience_weight
params.description: How important is this criteria to the matching score, range from 0 to 1.

params.name: locations_weight
params.description: How important is this criteria to the matching score, range from 0 to 1.

params.name: languages_weight
params.description: How important is this criteria to the matching score, range from 0 to 1.

params.name: skills_weight
params.description: How important is this criteria to the matching score, range from 0 to 1.

params.name: education_weight
params.description: How important is this criteria to the matching score, range from 0 to 1.

params.name: search_expression_weight
params.description: How important is this criteria to the matching score, range from 0 to 1.

params.name: soc_codes_weight
params.description: How important is this criteria to the matching score, range from 0 to 1.

params.name: management_level_weight
params.description: How important is this criteria to the matching score, range from 0 to 1.

items.docs:
Get the matching score between a resume and a job description. The score ranges between 0 and 1, with 0 being not a match at all, and 1 being perfect match.
Note, this score will not directly match the score returned from POST [/resume_search/details/{identifier}](#post-/resume_search/details/-identifier-).

Split / merge / rotate / delete pages of a document.
Documents with multiple pages can be splitted into multiple documents, or merged into one document.
Each page can also be rotated. Edit operations will trigger re-parsing of the documents involved.
